findings.title: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt cross-file suppression drops bugs in small/manual phases
findings.summary: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt tells diff reviewers to skip cross-file architectural concerns, but the `small` auto-phase (and manual `--phase small/medium/large`) has only diff reviewers, so cross-file bugs are silently dropped. Gate the skip clause to grouped-diff phases or retain cross-file coverage.
messages: [must] internal/agent/prompts.go (AutoPhaseDiffPrompt) — "Skip cross-file architectural concerns" silently breaks `small` auto-phase where a single diff reviewer is the ONLY reviewer; cross-file bugs dropped.

internal/agent/diff_review.go:30: `--role-prompts` applies to any non-empty phase, including manual `--phase small/medium/large`; those runs get the auto-phase subset prompt and can incorrectly skip cross-file issues.

internal/runner/runner.go:299: `RolePrompts` is propagated for every phased run including `small`; `parsePhases("small")` creates only diff reviewers, so small reviews can silently miss cross-file bugs.

internal/agent/prompts.go:178: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt tells diff reviewers to skip cross-file concerns because an arch reviewer handles them, but `small` creates only diff reviewers; enabling it can silently drop cross-file findings.

findings.title: RolePrompts CLI default silently overrides env/config precedence
findings.summary: In cmd/acr/main.go:495, `RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts` evaluates to false when neither flag is passed and overrides `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true` / `role_prompts: true`, violating the documented flags > env > config precedence. Set flagValues only when `RolePromptsSet` is true, mirroring AutoPhase.
messages: [must] cmd/acr/main.go:495 — `RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts` silently overrides `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true` when neither flag is passed; only set flagValues when `RolePromptsSet` is true, mirroring AutoPhase.
cmd/acr/main.go:495: `--no-role-prompts=false` still marks `RolePromptsSet` true at line 444, then this default-false expression overrides `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true` or `role_prompts: true` to false.

info.title: Reviewer process notes (non-actionable)
info.summary: Reviewer meta-commentary about how they planned to inspect the repo, memory paths, and cross-check the pasted diff against the working tree. No actionable findings.
messages: `oh-my-codex:code-review` に該当するため、レビュー用の手順だけ確認してから、実ファイルと設定解決の流れを読んで差分上のバグに絞ります。
レビュー出力はユーザー指定の `file:line: description` に合わせるため、skill の重い並列レビュー形式は採用せず、差分対象の実装経路だけを読みます。
現在の作業ツリーには提示 diff 周辺の実装とテストが既に入っているようなので、`config.Resolve` までつながる現在の行番号で確認しています。
差分だけで断定せず、`RolePrompts` がどこで解決され、runner に渡り、agent 側でどう使われるかを見ます。
現行ツリーには `role_prompts` 周辺の既知 sanity baseline もありますが、レビュー対象としては実コードの行番号に落とします。
I'll review the actual repository context around prompt resolution and config precedence, then report only concrete bugs from non-test files.
The diff appears to touch a feature that already exists in the working tree, so I'm checking the surrounding wiring rather than relying on the pasted patch alone.
I found extra role-prompt wiring in the working tree outside the pasted hunk, so I'm checking whether the pasted diff is complete.

findings.title: Small-phase role prompts issue is broader than any single file group
findings.summary: The arch finding about diff prompts skipping cross-file concerns is corroborated independently from prompt text, prompt selection, and runner propagation across multiple diff groups, showing the bug spans prompt content, agent prompt resolution, and phased runner behavior.

findings.title: Codex role-prompts no-op was not covered by diff findings
findings.summary: The arch group reports that codex reviewers bypass the prompt-resolution path, but no succeeding diff group reports a corresponding issue despite g01 covering internal/agent/diff_review.go, leaving this architectural concern uncorroborated at file-review level.

findings.title: Unknown phase fallback was not covered by diff findings
findings.summary: The arch group flags resolvePrompts treating any non-arch phase as diff, but no succeeding diff group reports or refutes that behavior even though g01 covered internal/agent/diff_review.go, leaving a cross-phase contract risk unaddressed by the targeted review.

findings.title: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt cross-file suppression also impacts manual --phase small/medium/large
findings.summary: Arch finding (id=2) framed the cross-file-skip regression as an auto-phase small-only issue. g01 (id=9) and g03 (id=12) confirm RolePrompts is selected for any non-arch phase and propagated by runner.go for manual --phase runs as well, so any small/medium/large invocation without an arch reviewer silently drops cross-file findings. Severity should escalate beyond the auto-phase-only framing because it affects the documented manual phase contract too.

findings.title: Codex reviewer no-op for --role-prompts not cross-validated by file-owning diff group
findings.summary: Arch (id=1) flagged that codex bypasses executeDiffBasedReview, making --role-prompts a silent no-op for codex. g01 owns internal/agent/diff_review.go and cmd/acr/main.go where the flag is exposed without agent-restriction, but produced no concrete confirmation of the codex routing gap. Coverage of the codex execution path is therefore unconfirmed at the diff-group level — a gap, not an absence of issues.

findings.title: Ref-file arch path coverage gap
findings.summary: Arch (id=5) raised that ref-file arch reviews may receive the generic Claude ref-file prompt with no arch framing — a pre-existing gap surfaced by this refactor. The ref-file path lives in internal/agent/diff_review.go (g01's territory), but g01 produced no concrete finding confirming or refuting the ref-file arch behavior. Cross-group coverage of the ref-file arch case is unverified.

findings.title: Widespread architectural flaw: 'small' phase cross-file blind spot
findings.summary: The design flaw where the 'small' auto-phase is incorrectly instructed to skip cross-file architectural concerns was independently detected by the arch review and all three diff groups across multiple files (prompts.go, diff_review.go, runner.go). This cross-layer detection confirms it as a pervasive systemic issue.

findings.title: Confirmed configuration precedence bug
findings.summary: Both the arch review and group g03 independently flagged the same boolean flag precedence bug in cmd/acr/main.go where environment variables are silently overridden by defaults. This corroboration confirms the bug's validity, even though it was missed by the group originally assigned to the file (g01).

findings.title: Unaddressed Codex agent prompt bypass
findings.summary: The arch review identified a blocking issue in internal/agent/diff_review.go where Codex agents silently bypass the role prompt logic. Group g01, assigned to review this file, failed to report or address this specific bypass.
